title: cordia guineensis subsp. guineensis
intro: Cordia guineensis subsp. guineensis, also known as the West African cordia, is a species of flowering plant in the bignonia family, characterized by its showy flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cordia guineensis subsp. guineensis include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as the protection against oxidative stress and inflammation. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed orally or used topically to promote wound healing and skin health.
